body: {"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"claude.md 'persona system' section lacks install precedence documentation (ac 13 gap): All five dependencies (ddx-06cbaa90, ddx-04566a8f, ddx-06f820f4, ddx-64ecd687, ddx-c10d5af2) are closed and their implementation is in place: install.go has --global at line 49, update.go routes through performGlobalUpdate(), init.go has initGlobal(), the registry has two-layer resolution in resolve.go, and ddx doctor reports both layers. ADR-027 (Accepted 2026-06-01), SKILL.md 'Install topology' section (lines 31-40), and reference/agents.md 'Install topology' section (lines 144-155) are all present. Child 1 owns the single runtime precedence entry point (registry.ResolvePlugin) plus the topology ADR, since every other slice resolves through it. Child 2 owns the machine-wide --global write surface for both install and update (ddxroot.GlobalDir + home-level harness links). Child 3 owns the no-flag project surface, where in-tree vs convention is handled implicitly by ddxroot.Path for both install and update. Child 4 owns the operator-facing entry points — ddx init global/project split and ddx doctor's two-layer report — plus the CLAUDE.md doc. Spec deliverable (parent AC13) is divided: the ADR rides with the precedence design (Child 1) and the Persona System precedence note rides with the operator/doctor slice (Child 4). The dep on ddx-06cbaa90 (home resolution) is preserved on every child because all install paths route through ddxroot.Path()/GlobalDir(). All three parent NON-SCOPE items (both modes coexist; no ~/.config/ddx migration; no harness paths beyond Claude Code + Codex) are carried into the relevant children. Spec updates (ADR/skill-topology + CLAUDE.md Persona System) landed in this bead's commit.","coverage":"Child 1 AC4 (ADR skill-install-topology + CLAUDE.md/cli-cmd-CLAUDE.md Persona System precedence) and Child 2 AC4 (library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md install-location docs)"}],"child_ids":["ddx-9ae1192f","ddx-6931d485","ddx-747f1b35","ddx-67b477d3"],"rationale":"Decomposed along the existing code seams discovered in the tree: (1) the runtime resolver cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:29 ResolvePlugin plus its governing ADR/CLAUDE.md docs form the foundation every other surface depends on; (2) the --global write surface spans install.go:49/184/202 and update.go:300 performGlobalUpdate, including agent-tier links and the SKILL.md doc; (3) the non-global project install must honor in-tree vs convention mode via ddxroot.Path() in install.go:195 and the default update path; (4) init.go:318 initGlobal, plain init's lazy behavior, and doctor.go:415 reportSkillInstallTopology cluster as the setup+reporting surface. Each child is independently testable with its own named Test* symbols and carries the shared go test (./cmd/... ./internal/registry/...) and lefthook gates. Spec/AC13 is split so the topology ADR + CLAUDE.md precedence land with the resolver (Child 1) and the SKILL.md install-location docs land with the global surface (Child 2). All four parent NON-SCOPE items (no per-project removal, no legacy ~/.config/ddx migration, no extra harness path discovery, both modes coexist) and the dep on ddx-06cbaa90 are preserved verbatim in every child; the ddx-777f4a09 ddx-validate hook-scoping constraint is carried in the two children that change install locations (Children 2 and 3)."}

Spec updates (ADR/skill-topology + CLAUDE.md Persona System) landed in this bead's commit","coverage":"child 'doctor + docs' AC 2"}],"child_ids":["ddx-fab52595","ddx-b418c28c","ddx-c0292631","ddx-7e585c0a"],"rationale":"Split along the natural seam between the runtime resolver, the install/update CLI surface, the init lifecycle, and the diagnostics+documentation layer. The registry three-layer lookup is foundational and independently testable (parent AC 4-5), so it is its own child with no CLI dependency. The install/update --global surface (parent AC 1-3, 8-9) consumes that resolver and shares one set of global/agent-tier link primitives, keeping in-tree vs convention vs global resolution in a single coherent bead. init --global (parent AC 6-7) reuses the install child's global+link primitives but covers a distinct lifecycle (umbrella setup vs lazy project init), so it depends on install/update. doctor+docs (parent AC 10, 13) reports the layers the prior three children create and lands the governing-artifact updates, so it depends on all of them. Every parent non-scope item (per-project install retained, ~/.config legacy migration, harness discovery beyond Claude Code/Codex) is preserved across the children's NON-SCOPE blocks, and the ddx-06cbaa90 dependency is carried on all four children since each routes through ddxroot.Path()/GlobalDir(). go test (AC 11) and lefthook (AC 12) gates are replicated into every child."}
